verses.esv: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
verses.kjv: PAUL, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, unto the church of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints which are in all Achaia:
verses.bsb: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,  To the church of God in Corinth, together with all the saints throughout Achaia:

verses.esv: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: Grace be to you and peace from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.esv: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort,
verses.kjv: Blessed be God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort;
verses.bsb: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of compassion and the God of all comfort,

verses.esv: who comforts us in all our affliction, so that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
verses.kjv: Who comforteth us in all our tribulation, that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.
verses.bsb: who comforts us in all our troubles, so that we can comfort those in any trouble with the comfort we ourselves have received from God.

verses.esv: For as we share abundantly in Christ's sufferings, so through Christ we share abundantly in comfort too.
verses.kjv: For as the sufferings of Christ abound in us, so our consolation also aboundeth by Christ.
verses.bsb: For just as the sufferings of Christ overflow to us, so also through Christ our comfort overflows.

verses.esv: If we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation; and if we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which you experience when you patiently endure the same sufferings that we suffer.
verses.kjv: And whether we be afflicted, it is for your consolation and salvation, which is effectual in the enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer: or whether we be comforted, it is for your consolation and salvation.
verses.bsb: If we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation; if we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which accomplishes in you patient endurance of the same sufferings we experience.

verses.esv: Our hope for you is unshaken, for we know that as you share in our sufferings, you will also share in our comfort.
verses.kjv: And our hope of you is stedfast, knowing, that as ye are partakers of the sufferings, so shall ye be also of the consolation.
verses.bsb: And our hope for you is sure, because we know that just as you share in our sufferings, so also you will share in our comfort.

verses.esv: For we do not want you to be unaware, brothers, of the affliction we experienced in Asia. For we were so utterly burdened beyond our strength that we despaired of life itself.
verses.kjv: For we would not, brethren, have you ignorant of our trouble which came to us in Asia, that we were pressed out of measure, above strength, insomuch that we despaired even of life:
verses.bsb: We do not want you to be unaware, brothers, of the hardships we encountered in the province of Asia. We were under a burden far beyond our ability to endure, so that we despaired even of life.

verses.esv: Indeed, we felt that we had received the sentence of death. But that was to make us rely not on ourselves but on God who raises the dead.
verses.kjv: But we had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God which raiseth the dead:
verses.bsb: Indeed, we felt we were under the sentence of death, in order that we would not trust in ourselves, but in God, who raises the dead.

verses.esv: He delivered us from such a deadly peril, and he will deliver us. On him we have set our hope that he will deliver us again.
verses.kjv: Who delivered us from so great a death, and doth deliver: in whom we trust that he will yet deliver us;
verses.bsb: He has delivered us from such a deadly peril, and He will deliver us. In Him we have placed our hope that He will yet again deliver us,

verses.esv: You also must help us by prayer, so that many will give thanks on our behalf for the blessing granted us through the prayers of many.
verses.kjv: Ye also helping together by prayer for us, that for the gift bestowed upon us by the means of many persons thanks may be given by many on our behalf.
verses.bsb: as you help us by your prayers. Then many will give thanks on our behalf for the favor shown us in answer to their prayers.

verses.esv: For our boast is this, the testimony of our conscience, that we behaved in the world with simplicity and godly sincerity, not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God, and supremely so toward you.
verses.kjv: For our rejoicing is this, the testimony of our conscience, that in simplicity and godly sincerity, not with fleshly wisdom, but by the grace of God, we have had our conversation in the world, and more abundantly to you-ward.
verses.bsb: And this is our boast: Our conscience testifies that we have conducted ourselves in the world, and especially in relation to you, in the holiness and sincerity that are from God—not in worldly wisdom, but in the grace of God.

verses.esv: For we are not writing to you anything other than what you read and understand and I hope you will fully understand—
verses.kjv: For we write none other things unto you, than what ye read or acknowledge; and I trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end;
verses.bsb: For we do not write you anything that is beyond your ability to read and understand. And I hope that you will understand us completely,

verses.esv: just as you did partially understand us—that on the day of our Lord Jesus you will boast of us as we will boast of you.
verses.kjv: As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also are ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.
verses.bsb: as you have already understood us in part, so that you may boast of us just as we will boast of you in the day of our Lord Jesus.

verses.esv: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to come to you first, so that you might have a second experience of grace.
verses.kjv: And in this confidence I was minded to come unto you before, that ye might have a second benefit;
verses.bsb: Confident of this, I planned to visit you first, so that you might receive a double blessing.

verses.esv: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and to come back to you from Macedonia and have you send me on my way to Judea.
verses.kjv: And to pass by you into Macedonia, and to come again out of Macedonia unto you, and of you to be brought on my way toward Judæa.
verses.bsb: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and to return to you from Macedonia, and then to have you help me on my way to Judea.

verses.esv: Was I vacillating when I wanted to do this? Do I make my plans according to the flesh, ready to say “Yes, yes” and “No, no” at the same time?
verses.kjv: When I therefore was thus minded, did I use lightness? or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be yea yea, and nay nay?
verses.bsb: When I planned this, did I do it carelessly? Or do I make my plans by human standards, so as to say “Yes, yes” when I really mean “No, no”?

verses.esv: As surely as God is faithful, our word to you has not been Yes and No.
verses.kjv: But as God is true, our word toward you was not yea and nay.
verses.bsb: But as surely as God is faithful, our message to you is not “Yes” and “No.”

verses.esv: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, whom we proclaimed among you, Silvanus and Timothy and I, was not Yes and No, but in him it is always Yes.
verses.kjv: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, even by me and Silvanus and Timotheus, was not yea and nay, but in him was yea.
verses.bsb: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was proclaimed among you by me and Silvanus and Timothy, was not “Yes” and “No,” but in Him it has always been “Yes.”

verses.esv: For all the promises of God find their Yes in him. That is why it is through him that we utter our Amen to God for his glory.
verses.kjv: For all the promises of God in him are yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us.
verses.bsb: For all the promises of God are “Yes” in Christ. And so through Him, our “Amen” is spoken to the glory of God.

verses.esv: And it is God who establishes us with you in Christ, and has anointed us,
verses.kjv: Now he which stablisheth us with you in Christ, and hath anointed us, is God;
verses.bsb: Now it is God who establishes both us and you in Christ. He anointed us,

verses.esv: and who has also put his seal on us and given us his Spirit in our hearts as a guarantee.
verses.kjv: Who hath also sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit in our hearts.
verses.bsb: placed His seal on us, and put His Spirit in our hearts as a pledge of what is to come.

verses.esv: But I call God to witness against me—it was to spare you that I refrained from coming again to Corinth.
verses.kjv: Moreover I call God for a record upon my soul, that to spare you I came not as yet unto Corinth.
verses.bsb: I call God as my witness that it was in order to spare you that I did not return to Corinth.

verses.esv: Not that we lord it over your faith, but we work with you for your joy, for you stand firm in your faith.
verses.kjv: Not for that we have dominion over your faith, but are helpers of your joy: for by faith ye stand.
verses.bsb: Not that we lord it over your faith, but we are fellow workers with you for your joy, because it is by faith that you stand firm.

verses.esv: For I made up my mind not to make another painful visit to you.
verses.kjv: BUT I determined this with myself, that I would not come again to you in heaviness.
verses.bsb: So I made up my mind not to make another painful visit to you.

verses.esv: For if I cause you pain, who is there to make me glad but the one whom I have pained?
verses.kjv: For if I make you sorry, who is he then that maketh me glad, but the same which is made sorry by me?
verses.bsb: For if I grieve you, who is left to cheer me but those whom I have grieved?

verses.esv: And I wrote as I did, so that when I came I might not suffer pain from those who should have made me rejoice, for I felt sure of all of you, that my joy would be the joy of you all.
verses.kjv: And I wrote this same unto you, lest, when I came, I should have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all, that my joy is the joy of you all.
verses.bsb: I wrote as I did so that on my arrival I would not be saddened by those who ought to make me rejoice. I had confidence in all of you, that you would share my joy.

verses.esv: For I wrote to you out of much affliction and anguish of heart and with many tears, not to cause you pain but to let you know the abundant love that I have for you.
verses.kjv: For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote unto you with many tears; not that ye should be grieved, but that ye might know the love which I have more abundantly unto you.
verses.bsb: For through many tears I wrote you out of great distress and anguish of heart, not to grieve you but to let you know how much I love you.

verses.esv: Now if anyone has caused pain, he has caused it not to me, but in some measure—not to put it too severely—to all of you.
verses.kjv: But if any have caused grief, he hath not grieved me, but in part: that I may not overcharge you all.
verses.bsb: Now if anyone has caused grief, he has not grieved me but all of you—to some degree, not to overstate it.

verses.esv: For such a one, this punishment by the majority is enough,
verses.kjv: Sufficient to such a man is this punishment, which was inflicted of many.
verses.bsb: The punishment imposed on him by the majority is sufficient for him.

verses.esv: so you should rather turn to forgive and comfort him, or he may be overwhelmed by excessive sorrow.
verses.kjv: So that contrariwise ye ought rather to forgive him, and comfort him, lest perhaps such a one should be swallowed up with overmuch sorrow.
verses.bsb: So instead, you ought to forgive and comfort him, so that he will not be overwhelmed by excessive sorrow.

verses.esv: So I beg you to reaffirm your love for him.
verses.kjv: Wherefore I beseech you that ye would confirm your love toward him.
verses.bsb: Therefore I urge you to reaffirm your love for him.

verses.esv: For this is why I wrote, that I might test you and know whether you are obedient in everything.
verses.kjv: For to this end also did I write, that I might know the proof of you, whether ye be obedient in all things.
verses.bsb: My purpose in writing you was to see if you would stand the test and be obedient in everything.

verses.esv: Anyone whom you forgive, I also forgive. Indeed, what I have forgiven, if I have forgiven anything, has been for your sake in the presence of Christ,
verses.kjv: To whom ye forgive any thing, I forgive also: for if I forgave any thing, to whom I forgave it, for your sakes forgave I it in the person of Christ;
verses.bsb: If you forgive anyone, I also forgive him. And if I have forgiven anything, I have forgiven it in the presence of Christ for your sake,

verses.esv: so that we would not be outwitted by Satan; for we are not ignorant of his designs.
verses.kjv: Lest Satan should get an advantage of us: for we are not ignorant of his devices.
verses.bsb: in order that Satan should not outwit us. For we are not unaware of his schemes.

verses.esv: When I came to Troas to preach the gospel of Christ, even though a door was opened for me in the Lord,
verses.kjv: Furthermore, when I came to Troas to preach Christ’s gospel, and a door was opened unto me of the Lord,
verses.bsb: Now when I went to Troas to preach the gospel of Christ and a door stood open for me in the Lord,

verses.esv: my spirit was not at rest because I did not find my brother Titus there. So I took leave of them and went on to Macedonia.
verses.kjv: I had no rest in my spirit, because I found not Titus my brother: but taking my leave of them, I went from thence into Macedonia.
verses.bsb: I had no peace in my spirit, because I did not find my brother Titus there. So I said goodbye to them and went on to Macedonia.

verses.esv: But thanks be to God, who in Christ always leads us in triumphal procession, and through us spreads the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere.
verses.kjv: Now thanks be unto God, which always causeth us to triumph in Christ, and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place.
verses.bsb: But thanks be to God, who always leads us triumphantly as captives in Christ and through us spreads everywhere the fragrance of the knowledge of Him.

verses.esv: For we are the aroma of Christ to God among those who are being saved and among those who are perishing,
verses.kjv: For we are unto God a sweet savour of Christ, in them that are saved, and in them that perish:
verses.bsb: For we are to God the sweet aroma of Christ among those who are being saved and those who are perishing.

verses.esv: to one a fragrance from death to death, to the other a fragrance from life to life. Who is sufficient for these things?
verses.kjv: To the one we are the savour of death unto death; and to the other the savour of life unto life. And who is sufficient for these things?
verses.bsb: To the one, we are an odor of death and demise; to the other, a fragrance that brings life. And who is qualified for such a task?

verses.esv: For we are not, like so many, peddlers of God's word, but as men of sincerity, as commissioned by God, in the sight of God we speak in Christ.
verses.kjv: For we are not as many, which corrupt the word of God: but as of sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God speak we in Christ.
verses.bsb: For we are not like so many others, who peddle the word of God for profit. On the contrary, in Christ we speak before God with sincerity, as men sent from God.

verses.esv: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? Or do we need, as some do, letters of recommendation to you, or from you?
verses.kjv: DO we begin again to commend ourselves? or need we, as some others, epistles of commendation to you, or letters of commendation from you?
verses.bsb: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? Or do we need, like some people, letters of recommendation to you or from you?

verses.esv: You yourselves are our letter of recommendation, written on our hearts, to be known and read by all.
verses.kjv: Ye are our epistle written in our hearts, known and read of all men:
verses.bsb: You yourselves are our letter, inscribed on our hearts, known and read by everyone.

verses.esv: And you show that you are a letter from Christ delivered by us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
verses.kjv: Forasmuch as ye are manifestly declared to be the epistle of Christ ministered by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tables of stone, but in fleshy tables of the heart.
verses.bsb: It is clear that you are a letter from Christ, the result of our ministry, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.

verses.esv: Such is the confidence that we have through Christ toward God.
verses.kjv: And such trust have we through Christ to God-ward:
verses.bsb: Such confidence before God is ours through Christ.

verses.esv: Not that we are sufficient in ourselves to claim anything as coming from us, but our sufficiency is from God,
verses.kjv: Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves; but our sufficiency is of God;
verses.bsb: Not that we are competent in ourselves to claim that anything comes from us, but our competence comes from God.

verses.esv: who has made us sufficient to be ministers of a new covenant, not of the letter but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
verses.kjv: Who also hath made us able ministers of the new testament; not of the letter, but of the spirit: for the letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life.
verses.bsb: And He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not of the letter but of the Spirit; for the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.

verses.esv: Now if the ministry of death, carved in letters on stone, came with such glory that the Israelites could not gaze at Moses' face because of its glory, which was being brought to an end,
verses.kjv: But if the ministration of death, written and engraven in stones, was glorious, so that the children of Israel could not stedfastly behold the face of Moses for the glory of his countenance; which glory was to be done away:
verses.bsb: Now if the ministry of death, which was engraved in letters on stone, came with such glory that the Israelites could not gaze at the face of Moses because of its fleeting glory,

verses.esv: will not the ministry of the Spirit have even more glory?
verses.kjv: How shall not the ministration of the spirit be rather glorious?
verses.bsb: will not the ministry of the Spirit be even more glorious?

verses.esv: For if there was glory in the ministry of condemnation, the ministry of righteousness must far exceed it in glory.
verses.kjv: For if the ministration of condemnation be glory, much more doth the ministration of righteousness exceed in glory.
verses.bsb: For if the ministry of condemnation was glorious, how much more glorious is the ministry of righteousness!

verses.esv: Indeed, in this case, what once had glory has come to have no glory at all, because of the glory that surpasses it.
verses.kjv: For even that which was made glorious had no glory in this respect, by reason of the glory that excelleth.
verses.bsb: Indeed, what was once glorious has no glory now in comparison to the glory that surpasses it.

verses.esv: For if what was being brought to an end came with glory, much more will what is permanent have glory.
verses.kjv: For if that which is done away was glorious, much more that which remaineth is glorious.
verses.bsb: For if what was fading away came with glory, how much greater is the glory of that which endures!

verses.esv: Since we have such a hope, we are very bold,
verses.kjv: Seeing then that we have such hope, we use great plainness of speech:
verses.bsb: Therefore, since we have such a hope, we are very bold.

verses.esv: not like Moses, who would put a veil over his face so that the Israelites might not gaze at the outcome of what was being brought to an end.
verses.kjv: And not as Moses, which put a vail over his face, that the children of Israel could not stedfastly look to the end of that which is abolished:
verses.bsb: We are not like Moses, who would put a veil over his face to keep the Israelites from gazing at the end of what was fading away.

verses.esv: But their minds were hardened. For to this day, when they read the old covenant, that same veil remains unlifted, because only through Christ is it taken away.
verses.kjv: But their minds were blinded: for until this day remaineth the same vail untaken away in the reading of the old testament; which vail is done away in Christ.
verses.bsb: But their minds were closed. For to this day the same veil remains at the reading of the old covenant. It has not been lifted, because only in Christ can it be removed.

verses.esv: Yes, to this day whenever Moses is read a veil lies over their hearts.
verses.kjv: But even unto this day, when Moses is read, the vail is upon their heart.
verses.bsb: And even to this day when Moses is read, a veil covers their hearts.

verses.esv: But when one turns to the Lord, the veil is removed.
verses.kjv: Nevertheless when it shall turn to the Lord, the vail shall be taken away.
verses.bsb: But whenever anyone turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.

verses.esv: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
verses.kjv: Now the Lord is that Spirit: and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.
verses.bsb: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.

verses.esv: And we all, with unveiled face, beholding the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into the same image from one degree of glory to another. For this comes from the Lord who is the Spirit.
verses.kjv: But we all, with open face beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image from glory to glory, even as by the Spirit of the Lord.
verses.bsb: And we, who with unveiled faces all reflect the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into His image with intensifying glory, which comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.

verses.esv: Therefore, having this ministry by the mercy of God, we do not lose heart.
verses.kjv: THEREFORE seeing we have this ministry, as we have received mercy, we faint not;
verses.bsb: Therefore, since God in His mercy has given us this ministry, we do not lose heart.

verses.esv: But we have renounced disgraceful, underhanded ways. We refuse to practice cunning or to tamper with God's word, but by the open statement of the truth we would commend ourselves to everyone's conscience in the sight of God.
verses.kjv: But have renounced the hidden things of dishonesty, not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God deceitfully; but by manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every man’s conscience in the sight of God.
verses.bsb: Instead, we have renounced secret and shameful ways. We do not practice deceit, nor do we distort the word of God. On the contrary, by open proclamation of the truth, we commend ourselves to every man’s conscience in the sight of God.

verses.esv: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are perishing.
verses.kjv: But if our gospel be hid, it is hid to them that are lost:
verses.bsb: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are perishing.

verses.esv: In their case the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelievers, to keep them from seeing the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God.
verses.kjv: In whom the god of this world hath blinded the minds of them which believe not, lest the light of the glorious gospel of Christ, who is the image of God, should shine unto them.
verses.bsb: The god of this age has blinded the minds of unbelievers so they cannot see the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God.

verses.esv: For what we proclaim is not ourselves, but Jesus Christ as Lord, with ourselves as your servants for Jesus' sake.
verses.kjv: For we preach not ourselves, but Christ Jesus the Lord; and ourselves your servants for Jesus’ sake.
verses.bsb: For we do not proclaim ourselves, but Jesus Christ as Lord, and ourselves as your servants for Jesus’ sake.

verses.esv: For God, who said, “Let light shine out of darkness,” has shone in our hearts to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: For God, who commanded the light to shine out of darkness, hath shined in our hearts, to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: For God, who said, “Let light shine out of darkness,” made His light shine in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.

verses.esv: But we have this treasure in jars of clay, to show that the surpassing power belongs to God and not to us.
verses.kjv: But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us.
verses.bsb: Now we have this treasure in jars of clay to show that this surpassingly great power is from God and not from us.

verses.esv: We are afflicted in every way, but not crushed; perplexed, but not driven to despair;
verses.kjv: We are troubled on every side, yet not distressed; we are perplexed, but not in despair;
verses.bsb: We are hard pressed on all sides, but not crushed; perplexed, but not in despair;

verses.esv: persecuted, but not forsaken; struck down, but not destroyed;
verses.kjv: Persecuted, but not forsaken; cast down, but not destroyed;
verses.bsb: persecuted, but not forsaken; struck down, but not destroyed.

verses.esv: always carrying in the body the death of Jesus, so that the life of Jesus may also be manifested in our bodies.
verses.kjv: Always bearing about in the body the dying of the Lord Jesus, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our body.
verses.bsb: We always carry around in our body the death of Jesus, so that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our body.

verses.esv: For we who live are always being given over to death for Jesus' sake, so that the life of Jesus also may be manifested in our mortal flesh.
verses.kjv: For we which live are alway delivered unto death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our mortal flesh.
verses.bsb: For we who are alive are always consigned to death for Jesus’ sake, so that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our mortal body.

verses.esv: So death is at work in us, but life in you.
verses.kjv: So then death worketh in us, but life in you.
verses.bsb: So then, death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.

verses.esv: Since we have the same spirit of faith according to what has been written, “I believed, and so I spoke,” we also believe, and so we also speak,
verses.kjv: We having the same spirit of faith, according as it is written, I believed, and therefore have I spoken; we also believe, and therefore speak;
verses.bsb: And in keeping with what is written: “I believed, therefore I have spoken,” we who have the same spirit of faith also believe and therefore speak,

verses.esv: knowing that he who raised the Lord Jesus will raise us also with Jesus and bring us with you into his presence.
verses.kjv: Knowing that he which raised up the Lord Jesus shall raise up us also by Jesus, and shall present us with you.
verses.bsb: knowing that the One who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus and present us with you in His presence.

verses.esv: For it is all for your sake, so that as grace extends to more and more people it may increase thanksgiving, to the glory of God.
verses.kjv: For all things are for your sakes, that the abundant grace might through the thanksgiving of many redound to the glory of God.
verses.bsb: All this is for your benefit, so that the grace that is extending to more and more people may overflow in thanksgiving, to the glory of God.

verses.esv: So we do not lose heart. Though our outer self is wasting away, our inner self is being renewed day by day.
verses.kjv: For which cause we faint not; but though our outward man perish, yet the inward man is renewed day by day.
verses.bsb: Therefore we do not lose heart. Though our outer self is wasting away, yet our inner self is being renewed day by day.

verses.esv: For this light momentary affliction is preparing for us an eternal weight of glory beyond all comparison,
verses.kjv: For our light affliction, which is but for a moment, worketh for us a far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory;
verses.bsb: For our light and momentary affliction is producing for us an eternal weight of glory that is far beyond comparison.

verses.esv: as we look not to the things that are seen but to the things that are unseen. For the things that are seen are transient, but the things that are unseen are eternal.
verses.kjv: While we look not at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen: for the things which are seen are temporal; but the things which are not seen are eternal.
verses.bsb: So we fix our eyes not on what is seen, but on what is unseen. For what is seen is temporary, but what is unseen is eternal.

verses.esv: For we know that if the tent that is our earthly home is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
verses.kjv: FOR we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
verses.bsb: Now we know that if the earthly tent we live in is dismantled, we have a building from God, an eternal house in heaven, not built by human hands.

verses.esv: For in this tent we groan, longing to put on our heavenly dwelling,
verses.kjv: For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:
verses.bsb: For in this tent we groan, longing to be clothed with our heavenly dwelling,

verses.esv: if indeed by putting it on we may not be found naked.
verses.kjv: If so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.
verses.bsb: because when we are clothed, we will not be found naked.

verses.esv: For while we are still in this tent, we groan, being burdened—not that we would be unclothed, but that we would be further clothed, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
verses.kjv: For we that are in this tabernacle do groan, being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that mortality might be swallowed up of life.
verses.bsb: So while we are in this tent, we groan under our burdens, because we do not wish to be unclothed but clothed, so that our mortality may be swallowed up by life.

verses.esv: He who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who has given us the Spirit as a guarantee.
verses.kjv: Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.
verses.bsb: And God has prepared us for this very purpose and has given us the Spirit as a pledge of what is to come.

verses.esv: So we are always of good courage. We know that while we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord,
verses.kjv: Therefore we are always confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:
verses.bsb: Therefore we are always confident, although we know that while we are at home in the body, we are away from the Lord.

verses.esv: for we walk by faith, not by sight.
verses.kjv: (For we walk by faith, not by sight:)
verses.bsb: For we walk by faith, not by sight.

verses.esv: Yes, we are of good courage, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord.
verses.kjv: We are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
verses.bsb: We are confident, then, and would prefer to be away from the body and at home with the Lord.

verses.esv: So whether we are at home or away, we make it our aim to please him.
verses.kjv: Wherefore we labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.
verses.bsb: So we aspire to please Him, whether we are here in this body or away from it.

verses.esv: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each one may receive what is due for what he has done in the body, whether good or evil.
verses.kjv: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad.
verses.bsb: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, that each one may receive his due for the things done in the body, whether good or bad.

verses.esv: Therefore, knowing the fear of the Lord, we persuade others. But what we are is known to God, and I hope it is known also to your conscience.
verses.kjv: Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are made manifest unto God; and I trust also are made manifest in your consciences.
verses.bsb: Therefore, since we know what it means to fear the Lord, we try to persuade men. What we are is clear to God, and I hope it is clear to your conscience as well.

verses.esv: We are not commending ourselves to you again but giving you cause to boast about us, so that you may be able to answer those who boast about outward appearance and not about what is in the heart.
verses.kjv: For we commend not ourselves again unto you, but give you occasion to glory on our behalf, that ye may have somewhat to answer them which glory in appearance, and not in heart.
verses.bsb: We are not commending ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an occasion to be proud of us, so that you can answer those who take pride in appearances rather than in the heart.

verses.esv: For if we are beside ourselves, it is for God; if we are in our right mind, it is for you.
verses.kjv: For whether we be beside ourselves, it is to God: or whether we be sober, it is for your cause.
verses.bsb: If we are out of our mind, it is for God; if we are of sound mind, it is for you.

verses.esv: For the love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: that one has died for all, therefore all have died;
verses.kjv: For the love of Christ constraineth us; because we thus judge, that if one died for all, then were all dead:
verses.bsb: For Christ’s love compels us, because we are convinced that One died for all, therefore all died.

verses.esv: and he died for all, that those who live might no longer live for themselves but for him who for their sake died and was raised.
verses.kjv: And that he died for all, that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves, but unto him which died for them, and rose again.
verses.bsb: And He died for all, that those who live should no longer live for themselves, but for Him who died for them and was raised again.

verses.esv: From now on, therefore, we regard no one according to the flesh. Even though we once regarded Christ according to the flesh, we regard him thus no longer.
verses.kjv: Wherefore henceforth know we no man after the flesh: yea, though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we him no more.
verses.bsb: So from now on we regard no one according to the flesh. Although we once regarded Christ in this way, we do so no longer.

verses.esv: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come.
verses.kjv: Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.
verses.bsb: Therefore if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away. Behold, the new has come!

verses.esv: All this is from God, who through Christ reconciled us to himself and gave us the ministry of reconciliation;
verses.kjv: And all things are of God, who hath reconciled us to himself by Jesus Christ, and hath given to us the ministry of reconciliation;
verses.bsb: All this is from God, who reconciled us to Himself through Christ and gave us the ministry of reconciliation:

verses.esv: that is, in Christ God was reconciling the world to himself, not counting their trespasses against them, and entrusting to us the message of reconciliation.
verses.kjv: To wit, that God was in Christ, reconciling the world unto himself, not imputing their trespasses unto them; and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation.
verses.bsb: that God was reconciling the world to Himself in Christ, not counting men’s trespasses against them. And He has committed to us the message of reconciliation.

verses.esv: Therefore, we are ambassadors for Christ, God making his appeal through us. We implore you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God.
verses.kjv: Now then we are ambassadors for Christ, as though God did beseech you by us: we pray you in Christ’s stead, be ye reconciled to God.
verses.bsb: Therefore we are ambassadors for Christ, as though God were making His appeal through us. We implore you on behalf of Christ: Be reconciled to God.

verses.esv: For our sake he made him to be sin who knew no sin, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.
verses.kjv: For he hath made him to be sin for us, who knew no sin; that we might be made the righteousness of God in him.
verses.bsb: God made Him who knew no sin to be sin on our behalf, so that in Him we might become the righteousness of God.

verses.esv: Working together with him, then, we appeal to you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
verses.kjv: WE then, as workers together with him, beseech you also that ye receive not the grace of God in vain.
verses.bsb: As God’s fellow workers, then, we urge you not to receive God’s grace in vain.

verses.esv: For he says, “In a favorable time I listened to you, and in a day of salvation I have helped you.” Behold, now is the favorable time; behold, now is the day of salvation.
verses.kjv: (For he saith, I have heard thee in a time accepted, and in the day of salvation have I succoured thee: behold, now is the accepted time; behold, now is the day of salvation.)
verses.bsb: For He says:  “In the time of favor I heard you, and in the day of salvation I helped you.”   Behold, now is the time of favor; now is the day of salvation!

verses.esv: We put no obstacle in anyone's way, so that no fault may be found with our ministry,
verses.kjv: Giving no offence in any thing, that the ministry be not blamed:
verses.bsb: We put no obstacle in anyone’s way, so that no one can discredit our ministry.

verses.esv: but as servants of God we commend ourselves in every way: by great endurance, in afflictions, hardships, calamities,
verses.kjv: But in all things approving ourselves as the ministers of God, in much patience, in afflictions, in necessities, in distresses,
verses.bsb: Rather, as servants of God we commend ourselves in every way: in great endurance; in troubles, hardships, and calamities;

verses.esv: beatings, imprisonments, riots, labors, sleepless nights, hunger;
verses.kjv: In stripes, in imprisonments, in tumults, in labours, in watchings, in fastings;
verses.bsb: in beatings, imprisonments, and riots; in labor, sleepless nights, and hunger;

verses.esv: by purity, knowledge, patience, kindness, the Holy Spirit, genuine love;
verses.kjv: By pureness, by knowledge, by longsuffering, by kindness, by the Holy Ghost, by love unfeigned,
verses.bsb: in purity, knowledge, patience, and kindness; in the Holy Spirit and in sincere love;

verses.esv: by truthful speech, and the power of God; with the weapons of righteousness for the right hand and for the left;
verses.kjv: By the word of truth, by the power of God, by the armour of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,
verses.bsb: in truthful speech and in the power of God; with the weapons of righteousness in the right hand and in the left;

verses.esv: through honor and dishonor, through slander and praise. We are treated as impostors, and yet are true;
verses.kjv: By honour and dishonour, by evil report and good report: as deceivers, and yet true;
verses.bsb: through glory and dishonor, slander and praise; viewed as imposters, yet genuine;

verses.esv: as unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and behold, we live; as punished, and yet not killed;
verses.kjv: As unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and, behold, we live; as chastened, and not killed;
verses.bsb: as unknown, yet well-known; dying, and yet we live on; punished, yet not killed;

verses.esv: as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, yet possessing everything.
verses.kjv: As sorrowful, yet alway rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.
verses.bsb: sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; poor, yet making many rich; having nothing, and yet possessing everything.

verses.esv: We have spoken freely to you, Corinthians; our heart is wide open.
verses.kjv: O ye Corinthians, our mouth is open unto you, our heart is enlarged.
verses.bsb: We have spoken freely to you, Corinthians. Our hearts are open wide.

verses.esv: You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted in your own affections.
verses.kjv: Ye are not straitened in us, but ye are straitened in your own bowels.
verses.bsb: It is not our affection, but yours, that is restrained.

verses.esv: In return (I speak as to children) widen your hearts also.
verses.kjv: Now for a recompence in the same, (I speak as unto my children,) be ye also enlarged.
verses.bsb: As a fair exchange, I ask you as my children: Open wide your hearts also.

verses.esv: Do not be unequally yoked with unbelievers. For what partnership has righteousness with lawlessness? Or what fellowship has light with darkness?
verses.kjv: Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers: for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness? and what communion hath light with darkness?
verses.bsb: Do not be unequally yoked with unbelievers. For what partnership can righteousness have with wickedness? Or what fellowship does light have with darkness?

verses.esv: What accord has Christ with Belial? Or what portion does a believer share with an unbeliever?
verses.kjv: And what concord hath Christ with Belial? or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel?
verses.bsb: What harmony is there between Christ and Belial? Or what does a believer have in common with an unbeliever?

verses.esv: What agreement has the temple of God with idols? For we are the temple of the living God; as God said, “I will make my dwelling among them and walk among them, and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
verses.kjv: And what agreement hath the temple of God with idols? for ye are the temple of the living God; as God hath said, I will dwell in them, and walk in them; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
verses.bsb: What agreement can exist between the temple of God and idols? For we are the temple of the living God. As God has said:  “I will dwell with them and walk among them, and I will be their God, and they will be My people.”

verses.esv: Therefore go out from their midst, and be separate from them, says the Lord, and touch no unclean thing; then I will welcome you,
verses.kjv: Wherefore come out from among them, and be ye separate, saith the Lord, and touch not the unclean thing; and I will receive you,
verses.bsb: “Therefore come out from among them and be separate, says the Lord. Touch no unclean thing, and I will receive you.”

verses.esv: and I will be a father to you, and you shall be sons and daughters to me, says the Lord Almighty.” Since we have these promises, beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from every defilement of body and spirit, bringing holiness to completion in the fear of God.
verses.kjv: And will be a Father unto you, and ye shall be my sons and daughters, saith the Lord Almighty.
verses.bsb: And:  “I will be a Father to you, and you will be My sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty.”

verses.esv: Make room in your hearts for us. We have wronged no one, we have corrupted no one, we have taken advantage of no one.
verses.kjv: Receive us; we have wronged no man, we have corrupted no man, we have defrauded no man.
verses.bsb: Make room for us in your hearts. We have wronged no one, we have corrupted no one, we have exploited no one.

verses.esv: I do not say this to condemn you, for I said before that you are in our hearts, to die together and to live together.
verses.kjv: I speak not this to condemn you: for I have said before, that ye are in our hearts to die and live with you.
verses.bsb: I do not say this to condemn you. I have said before that you so occupy our hearts that we live and die together with you.

verses.esv: I am acting with great boldness toward you; I have great pride in you; I am filled with comfort. In all our affliction, I am overflowing with joy.
verses.kjv: Great is my boldness of speech toward you, great is my glorying of you: I am filled with comfort, I am exceeding joyful in all our tribulation.
verses.bsb: Great is my confidence in you; great is my pride in you; I am filled with encouragement; in all our troubles my joy overflows.

verses.esv: For even when we came into Macedonia, our bodies had no rest, but we were afflicted at every turn—fighting without and fear within.
verses.kjv: For, when we were come into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we were troubled on every side; without were fightings, within were fears.
verses.bsb: For when we arrived in Macedonia, our bodies had no rest, but we were pressed from every direction—conflicts on the outside, fears within.

verses.esv: But God, who comforts the downcast, comforted us by the coming of Titus,
verses.kjv: Nevertheless God, that comforteth those that are cast down, comforted us by the coming of Titus;
verses.bsb: But God, who comforts the downcast, comforted us by the arrival of Titus,

verses.esv: and not only by his coming but also by the comfort with which he was comforted by you, as he told us of your longing, your mourning, your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.
verses.kjv: And not by his coming only, but by the consolation wherewith he was comforted in you, when he told us your earnest desire, your mourning, your fervent mind toward me; so that I rejoiced the more.
verses.bsb: and not only by his arrival, but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your longing, your mourning, and your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced all the more.

verses.esv: For even if I made you grieve with my letter, I do not regret it—though I did regret it, for I see that that letter grieved you, though only for a while.
verses.kjv: For though I made you sorry with a letter, I do not repent, though I did repent: for I perceive that the same epistle hath made you sorry, though it were but for a season.
verses.bsb: Even if I caused you sorrow by my letter, I do not regret it. Although I did regret it, I now see that my letter caused you sorrow, but only for a short time.

verses.esv: As it is, I rejoice, not because you were grieved, but because you were grieved into repenting. For you felt a godly grief, so that you suffered no loss through us.
verses.kjv: Now I rejoice, not that ye were made sorry, but that ye sorrowed to repentance: for ye were made sorry after a godly manner, that ye might receive damage by us in nothing.
verses.bsb: And now I rejoice, not because you were made sorrowful, but because your sorrow led you to repentance. For you felt the sorrow that God had intended, and so were not harmed in any way by us.

verses.esv: For godly grief produces a repentance that leads to salvation without regret, whereas worldly grief produces death.
verses.kjv: For godly sorrow worketh repentance to salvation not to be repented of: but the sorrow of the world worketh death.
verses.bsb: Godly sorrow brings repentance that leads to salvation without regret, but worldly sorrow brings death.

verses.esv: For see what earnestness this godly grief has produced in you, but also what eagerness to clear yourselves, what indignation, what fear, what longing, what zeal, what punishment! At every point you have proved yourselves innocent in the matter.
verses.kjv: For behold this selfsame thing, that ye sorrowed after a godly sort, what carefulness it wrought in you, yea, what clearing of yourselves, yea, what indignation, yea, what fear, yea, what vehement desire, yea, what zeal, yea, what revenge! In all things ye have approved yourselves to be clear in this matter.
verses.bsb: Consider what this godly sorrow has produced in you: what earnestness, what eagerness to clear yourselves, what indignation, what alarm, what longing, what zeal, what vindication! In every way you have proved yourselves to be innocent in this matter.

verses.esv: So although I wrote to you, it was not for the sake of the one who did the wrong, nor for the sake of the one who suffered the wrong, but in order that your earnestness for us might be revealed to you in the sight of God.
verses.kjv: Wherefore, though I wrote unto you, I did it not for his cause that had done the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered wrong, but that our care for you in the sight of God might appear unto you.
verses.bsb: So even though I wrote to you, it was not on account of the one who did wrong or the one who was harmed, but rather that your earnestness on our behalf would be made clear to you in the sight of God.

verses.esv: Therefore we are comforted. And besides our own comfort, we rejoiced still more at the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.
verses.kjv: Therefore we were comforted in your comfort: yea, and exceedingly the more joyed we for the joy of Titus, because his spirit was refreshed by you all.
verses.bsb: On account of this, we are encouraged.  In addition to our own encouragement, we were even more delighted by the joy of Titus. For his spirit has been refreshed by all of you.

verses.esv: For whatever boasts I made to him about you, I was not put to shame. But just as everything we said to you was true, so also our boasting before Titus has proved true.
verses.kjv: For if I have boasted any thing to him of you, I am not ashamed; but as we spake all things to you in truth, even so our boasting, which I made before Titus, is found a truth.
verses.bsb: Indeed, I was not embarrassed by anything I had boasted to him about you. But just as everything we said to you was true, so our boasting to Titus has proved to be true as well.

verses.esv: And his affection for you is even greater, as he remembers the obedience of you all, how you received him with fear and trembling.
verses.kjv: And his inward affection is more abundant toward you, whilst he remembereth the obedience of you all, how with fear and trembling ye received him.
verses.bsb: And his affection for you is even greater when he remembers that you were all obedient as you welcomed him with fear and trembling.

verses.esv: I rejoice, because I have complete confidence in you.
verses.kjv: I rejoice therefore that I have confidence in you in all things.
verses.bsb: I rejoice that I can have complete confidence in you.

verses.esv: We want you to know, brothers, about the grace of God that has been given among the churches of Macedonia,
verses.kjv: MOREOVER, brethren, we do you to wit of the grace of God bestowed on the churches of Macedonia;
verses.bsb: Now, brothers, we want you to know about the grace that God has given the churches of Macedonia.

verses.esv: for in a severe test of affliction, their abundance of joy and their extreme poverty have overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part.
verses.kjv: How that in a great trial of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded unto the riches of their liberality.
verses.bsb: In the terrible ordeal they suffered, their abundant joy and deep poverty overflowed into rich generosity.

verses.esv: For they gave according to their means, as I can testify, and beyond their means, of their own accord,
verses.kjv: For to their power, I bear record, yea, and beyond their power they were willing of themselves;
verses.bsb: For I testify that they gave according to their ability and even beyond it. Of their own accord,

verses.esv: begging us earnestly for the favor of taking part in the relief of the saints—
verses.kjv: Praying us with much intreaty that we would receive the gift, and take upon us the fellowship of the ministering to the saints.
verses.bsb: they earnestly pleaded with us for the privilege of sharing in this service to the saints.

verses.esv: and this, not as we expected, but they gave themselves first to the Lord and then by the will of God to us.
verses.kjv: And this they did, not as we hoped, but first gave their own selves to the Lord, and unto us by the will of God.
verses.bsb: And not only did they do as we expected, but they gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, because it was the will of God.

verses.esv: Accordingly, we urged Titus that as he had started, so he should complete among you this act of grace.
verses.kjv: Insomuch that we desired Titus, that as he had begun, so he would also finish in you the same grace also.
verses.bsb: So we urged Titus to help complete your act of grace, just as he had started it.

verses.esv: But as you excel in everything—in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in all earnestness, and in our love for you—see that you excel in this act of grace also.
verses.kjv: Therefore, as ye abound in every thing, in faith, and utterance, and knowledge, and in all diligence, and in your love to us, see that ye abound in this grace also.
verses.bsb: But just as you excel in everything—in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in complete earnestness, and in the love we inspired in you—see that you also excel in this grace of giving.

verses.esv: I say this not as a command, but to prove by the earnestness of others that your love also is genuine.
verses.kjv: I speak not by commandment, but by occasion of the forwardness of others, and to prove the sincerity of your love.
verses.bsb: I am not making a demand, but I am testing the sincerity of your love in comparison to the earnestness of others.

verses.esv: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sake he became poor, so that you by his poverty might become rich.
verses.kjv: For ye know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that ye through his poverty might be rich.
verses.bsb: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though He was rich, yet for your sakes He became poor, so that you through His poverty might become rich.

verses.esv: And in this matter I give my judgment: this benefits you, who a year ago started not only to do this work but also to desire to do it.
verses.kjv: And herein I give my advice: for this is expedient for you, who have begun before, not only to do, but also to be forward a year ago.
verses.bsb: And this is my opinion about what is helpful for you in this matter: Last year you were the first not only to give, but even to have such a desire.

verses.esv: So now finish doing it as well, so that your readiness in desiring it may be matched by your completing it out of what you have.
verses.kjv: Now therefore perform the doing of it; that as there was a readiness to will, so there may be a performance also out of that which ye have.
verses.bsb: Now finish the work, so that you may complete it just as eagerly as you began, according to your means.

verses.esv: For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what a person has, not according to what he does not have.
verses.kjv: For if there be first a willing mind, it is accepted according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not.
verses.bsb: For if the eagerness is there, the gift is acceptable according to what one has, not according to what he does not have.

verses.esv: For I do not mean that others should be eased and you burdened, but that as a matter of fairness
verses.kjv: For I mean not that other men be eased, and ye burdened:
verses.bsb: It is not our intention that others may be relieved while you are burdened, but that there may be equality.

verses.esv: your abundance at the present time should supply their need, so that their abundance may supply your need, that there may be fairness.
verses.kjv: But by an equality, that now at this time your abundance may be a supply for their want, that their abundance also may be a supply for your want: that there may be equality:
verses.bsb: At the present time, your surplus will meet their need, so that in turn their surplus will meet your need. Then there will be equality.

verses.esv: As it is written, “Whoever gathered much had nothing left over, and whoever gathered little had no lack.”
verses.kjv: As it is written, He that had gathered much had nothing over; and he that had gathered little had no lack.
verses.bsb: As it is written:  “He who gathered much had no excess, and he who gathered little had no shortfall.”

verses.esv: But thanks be to God, who put into the heart of Titus the same earnest care I have for you.
verses.kjv: But thanks be to God, which put the same earnest care into the heart of Titus for you.
verses.bsb: But thanks be to God, who put into the heart of Titus the same devotion I have for you.

verses.esv: For he not only accepted our appeal, but being himself very earnest he is going to you of his own accord.
verses.kjv: For indeed he accepted the exhortation; but being more forward, of his own accord he went unto you.
verses.bsb: For not only did he welcome our appeal, but he is eagerly coming to you of his own volition.

verses.esv: With him we are sending the brother who is famous among all the churches for his preaching of the gospel.
verses.kjv: And we have sent with him the brother, whose praise is in the gospel throughout all the churches;
verses.bsb: Along with Titus we are sending the brother who is praised by all the churches for his work in the gospel.

verses.esv: And not only that, but he has been appointed by the churches to travel with us as we carry out this act of grace that is being ministered by us, for the glory of the Lord himself and to show our good will.
verses.kjv: And not that only, but who was also chosen of the churches to travel with us with this grace, which is administered by us to the glory of the same Lord, and declaration of your ready mind:
verses.bsb: More than that, this brother was chosen by the churches to accompany us with the offering—the gracious gift we administer to honor the Lord Himself and to show our eagerness to help.

verses.esv: We take this course so that no one should blame us about this generous gift that is being administered by us,
verses.kjv: Avoiding this, that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us:
verses.bsb: We hope to avoid any criticism of the way we administer this generous gift.

verses.esv: for we aim at what is honorable not only in the Lord's sight but also in the sight of man.
verses.kjv: Providing for honest things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.
verses.bsb: For we are taking great care to do what is right, not only in the eyes of the Lord, but also in the eyes of men.

verses.esv: And with them we are sending our brother whom we have often tested and found earnest in many matters, but who is now more earnest than ever because of his great confidence in you.
verses.kjv: And we have sent with them our brother, whom we have oftentimes proved diligent in many things, but now much more diligent, upon the great confidence which I have in you.
verses.bsb: And we are sending along with them our brother whose earnestness has been proven many times and in many ways, and now even more so by his great confidence in you.

verses.esv: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for your benefit. And as for our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, the glory of Christ.
verses.kjv: Whether any do inquire of Titus, he is my partner and fellowhelper concerning you: or our brethren be inquired of, they are the messengers of the churches, and the glory of Christ.
verses.bsb: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker among you. As for our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, to the glory of Christ.

verses.esv: So give proof before the churches of your love and of our boasting about you to these men.
verses.kjv: Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the churches, the proof of your love, and of our boasting on your behalf.
verses.bsb: In full view of the churches, then, show these men the proof of your love and the reason for our boasting about you.

verses.esv: Now it is superfluous for me to write to you about the ministry for the saints,
verses.kjv: FOR as touching the ministering to the saints, it is superfluous for me to write to you:
verses.bsb: Now about the service to the saints, there is no need for me to write to you.

verses.esv: for I know your readiness, of which I boast about you to the people of Macedonia, saying that Achaia has been ready since last year. And your zeal has stirred up most of them.
verses.kjv: For I know the forwardness of your mind, for which I boast of you to them of Macedonia, that Achaia was ready a year ago; and your zeal hath provoked very many.
verses.bsb: For I know your eagerness to help, and I have been boasting to the Macedonians that since last year you in Achaia were prepared to give. And your zeal has stirred most of them to do likewise.

verses.esv: But I am sending the brothers so that our boasting about you may not prove empty in this matter, so that you may be ready, as I said you would be.
verses.kjv: Yet have I sent the brethren, lest our boasting of you should be in vain in this behalf; that, as I said, ye may be ready:
verses.bsb: But I am sending the brothers in order that our boasting about you in this matter should not prove empty, but that you will be prepared, just as I said.

verses.esv: Otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find that you are not ready, we would be humiliated—to say nothing of you—for being so confident.
verses.kjv: Lest haply if they of Macedonia come with me, and find you unprepared, we (that we say not, ye) should be ashamed in this same confident boasting.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, if any Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we—to say nothing of you—would be ashamed of having been so confident.

verses.esv: So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you and arrange in advance for the gift you have promised, so that it may be ready as a willing gift, not as an exaction.
verses.kjv: Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren, that they would go before unto you, and make up beforehand your bounty, whereof ye had notice before, that the same might be ready, as a matter of bounty, and not as of covetousness.
verses.bsb: So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to visit you beforehand and make arrangements for the bountiful gift you had promised. This way, your gift will be prepared generously and not begrudgingly.

verses.esv: The point is this: whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.
verses.kjv: But this I say, He which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully.
verses.bsb: Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously.

verses.esv: Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
verses.kjv: Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver.
verses.bsb: Each one should give what he has decided in his heart to give, not out of regret or compulsion. For God loves a cheerful giver.

verses.esv: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that having all sufficiency in all things at all times, you may abound in every good work.
verses.kjv: And God is able to make all grace abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work:
verses.bsb: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that in all things, at all times, having all that you need, you will abound in every good work.

verses.esv: As it is written, “He has distributed freely, he has given to the poor; his righteousness endures forever.”
verses.kjv: (As it is written, He hath dispersed abroad; he hath given to the poor: his righteousness remaineth for ever.
verses.bsb: As it is written:  “He has scattered abroad His gifts to the poor; His righteousness endures forever.”

verses.esv: He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness.
verses.kjv: Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food, and multiply your seed sown, and increase the fruits of your righteousness;)
verses.bsb: Now He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your store of seed and will increase the harvest of your righteousness.

verses.esv: You will be enriched in every way to be generous in every way, which through us will produce thanksgiving to God.
verses.kjv: Being enriched in every thing to all bountifulness, which causeth through us thanksgiving to God.
verses.bsb: You will be enriched in every way to be generous on every occasion, so that through us your giving will produce thanksgiving to God.

verses.esv: For the ministry of this service is not only supplying the needs of the saints but is also overflowing in many thanksgivings to God.
verses.kjv: For the administration of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints, but is abundant also by many thanksgivings unto God;
verses.bsb: For this ministry of service is not only supplying the needs of the saints, but is also overflowing in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.

verses.esv: By their approval of this service, they will glorify God because of your submission that comes from your confession of the gospel of Christ, and the generosity of your contribution for them and for all others,
verses.kjv: Whiles by the experiment of this ministration they glorify God for your professed subjection unto the gospel of Christ, and for your liberal distribution unto them, and unto all men;
verses.bsb: Because of the proof this ministry provides, the saints will glorify God for your obedient confession of the gospel of Christ, and for the generosity of your contribution to them and to all the others.

verses.esv: while they long for you and pray for you, because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.
verses.kjv: And by their prayer for you, which long after you for the exceeding grace of God in you.
verses.bsb: And their prayers for you will express their affection for you, because of the surpassing grace God has given you.

verses.esv: Thanks be to God for his inexpressible gift!
verses.kjv: Thanks be unto God for his unspeakable gift.
verses.bsb: Thanks be to God for His indescribable gift!

verses.esv: I, Paul, myself entreat you, by the meekness and gentleness of Christ—I who am humble when face to face with you, but bold toward you when I am away!—
verses.kjv: NOW I Paul myself beseech you by the meekness and gentleness of Christ, who in presence am base among you, but being absent am bold toward you:
verses.bsb: Now by the mildness and gentleness of Christ, I appeal to you—I, Paul, who am humble when face to face with you, but bold when away.

verses.esv: I beg of you that when I am present I may not have to show boldness with such confidence as I count on showing against some who suspect us of walking according to the flesh.
verses.kjv: But I beseech you, that I may not be bold when I am present with that confidence, wherewith I think to be bold against some, which think of us as if we walked according to the flesh.
verses.bsb: I beg you that when I come I may not need to be as bold as I expect toward those who presume that we live according to the flesh.

verses.esv: For though we walk in the flesh, we are not waging war according to the flesh.
verses.kjv: For though we walk in the flesh, we do not war after the flesh:
verses.bsb: For though we live in the flesh, we do not wage war according to the flesh.

verses.esv: For the weapons of our warfare are not of the flesh but have divine power to destroy strongholds.
verses.kjv: (For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds;)
verses.bsb: The weapons of our warfare are not the weapons of the world. Instead, they have divine power to demolish strongholds.

verses.esv: We destroy arguments and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God, and take every thought captive to obey Christ,
verses.kjv: Casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ;
verses.bsb: We tear down arguments and every presumption set up against the knowledge of God; and we take captive every thought to make it obedient to Christ.

verses.esv: being ready to punish every disobedience, when your obedience is complete.
verses.kjv: And having in a readiness to revenge all disobedience, when your obedience is fulfilled.
verses.bsb: And we will be ready to punish every act of disobedience, as soon as your obedience is complete.

verses.esv: Look at what is before your eyes. If anyone is confident that he is Christ's, let him remind himself that just as he is Christ's, so also are we.
verses.kjv: Do ye look on things after the outward appearance? If any man trust to himself that he is Christ’s, let him of himself think this again, that, as he is Christ’s, even so are we Christ’s.
verses.bsb: You are looking at outward appearances. If anyone is confident that he belongs to Christ, he should remind himself that we belong to Christ just as much as he does.

verses.esv: For even if I boast a little too much of our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up and not for destroying you, I will not be ashamed.
verses.kjv: For though I should boast somewhat more of our authority, which the Lord hath given us for edification, and not for your destruction, I should not be ashamed:
verses.bsb: For even if I boast somewhat excessively about the authority the Lord gave us for building you up rather than tearing you down, I will not be ashamed.

verses.esv: I do not want to appear to be frightening you with my letters.
verses.kjv: That I may not seem as if I would terrify you by letters.
verses.bsb: I do not want to seem to be trying to frighten you by my letters.

verses.esv: For they say, “His letters are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech of no account.”
verses.kjv: For his letters, say they, are weighty and powerful; but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech contemptible.
verses.bsb: For some say, “His letters are weighty and forceful, but his physical presence is unimpressive, and his speaking is of no account.”

verses.esv: Let such a person understand that what we say by letter when absent, we do when present.
verses.kjv: Let such an one think this, that, such as we are in word by letters when we are absent, such will we be also in deed when we are present.
verses.bsb: Such people should consider that what we are in our letters when absent, we will be in our actions when present.

verses.esv: Not that we dare to classify or compare ourselves with some of those who are commending themselves. But when they measure themselves by one another and compare themselves with one another, they are without understanding.
verses.kjv: For we dare not make ourselves of the number, or compare ourselves with some that commend themselves: but they measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves among themselves, are not wise.
verses.bsb: We do not dare to classify or compare ourselves with some who commend themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves and compare themselves with themselves, they show their ignorance.

verses.esv: But we will not boast beyond limits, but will boast only with regard to the area of influence God assigned to us, to reach even to you.
verses.kjv: But we will not boast of things without our measure, but according to the measure of the rule which God hath distributed to us, a measure to reach even unto you.
verses.bsb: We, however, will not boast beyond our limits, but only within the field of influence that God has assigned to us—a field that reaches even to you.

verses.esv: For we are not overextending ourselves, as though we did not reach you. For we were the first to come all the way to you with the gospel of Christ.
verses.kjv: For we stretch not ourselves beyond our measure, as though we reached not unto you: for we are come as far as to you also in preaching the gospel of Christ:
verses.bsb: We are not overstepping our bounds, as if we had not come to you. Indeed, we were the first to reach you with the gospel of Christ.

verses.esv: We do not boast beyond limit in the labors of others. But our hope is that as your faith increases, our area of influence among you may be greatly enlarged,
verses.kjv: Not boasting of things without our measure, that is, of other men’s labours; but having hope, when your faith is increased, that we shall be enlarged by you according to our rule abundantly,
verses.bsb: Neither do we boast beyond our limits in the labors of others. But we hope that as your faith increases, our area of influence among you will greatly increase as well,

verses.esv: so that we may preach the gospel in lands beyond you, without boasting of work already done in another's area of influence.
verses.kjv: To preach the gospel in the regions beyond you, and not to boast in another man’s line of things made ready to our hand.
verses.bsb: so that we can preach the gospel in the regions beyond you. Then we will not be boasting in the work already done in another man’s territory.

verses.esv: “Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.”
verses.kjv: But he that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Rather, “Let him who boasts boast in the Lord.”

verses.esv: For it is not the one who commends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord commends.
verses.kjv: For not he that commendeth himself is approved, but whom the Lord commendeth.
verses.bsb: For it is not the one who commends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord commends.

verses.esv: I wish you would bear with me in a little foolishness. Do bear with me!
verses.kjv: WOULD to God ye could bear with me a little in my folly: and indeed bear with me.
verses.bsb: I hope you will bear with a little of my foolishness, but you are already doing that.

verses.esv: For I feel a divine jealousy for you, since I betrothed you to one husband, to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
verses.kjv: For I am jealous over you with godly jealousy: for I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
verses.bsb: I am jealous for you with a godly jealousy. For I promised you to one husband, to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.

verses.esv: But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning, your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
verses.kjv: But I fear, lest by any means, as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.
verses.bsb: I am afraid, however, that just as Eve was deceived by the serpent’s cunning, your minds may be led astray from your simple and pure devotion to Christ.

verses.esv: For if someone comes and proclaims another Jesus than the one we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or if you accept a different gospel from the one you accepted, you put up with it readily enough.
verses.kjv: For if he that cometh preacheth another Jesus, whom we have not preached, or if ye receive another spirit, which ye have not received, or another gospel, which ye have not accepted, ye might well bear with him.
verses.bsb: For if someone comes and proclaims a Jesus other than the One we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit than the One you received, or a different gospel than the one you accepted, you put up with it way too easily.

verses.esv: Indeed, I consider that I am not in the least inferior to these super-apostles.
verses.kjv: For I suppose I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.
verses.bsb: I consider myself in no way inferior to those “super-apostles.”

verses.esv: Even if I am unskilled in speaking, I am not so in knowledge; indeed, in every way we have made this plain to you in all things.
verses.kjv: But though I be rude in speech, yet not in knowledge; but we have been throughly made manifest among you in all things.
verses.bsb: Although I am not a polished speaker, I am certainly not lacking in knowledge. We have made this clear to you in every way possible.

verses.esv: Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I preached God's gospel to you free of charge?
verses.kjv: Have I committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted, because I have preached to you the gospel of God freely?
verses.bsb: Was it a sin for me to humble myself in order to exalt you, because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?

verses.esv: I robbed other churches by accepting support from them in order to serve you.
verses.kjv: I robbed other churches, taking wages of them, to do you service.
verses.bsb: I robbed other churches by accepting their support in order to serve you.

verses.esv: And when I was with you and was in need, I did not burden anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia supplied my need. So I refrained and will refrain from burdening you in any way.
verses.kjv: And when I was present with you, and wanted, I was chargeable to no man: for that which was lacking to me the brethren which came from Macedonia supplied: and in all things I have kept myself from being burdensome unto you, and so will I keep myself.
verses.bsb: And when I was with you and in need, I was not a burden to anyone; for the brothers who came from Macedonia supplied my needs. I have refrained from being a burden to you in any way, and I will continue to do so.

verses.esv: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boasting of mine will not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.
